2025::Scoticia Osborne.managerContext:
What Scoti put 

1. Think back to your business goals for 2024. Highlight the 2-3 business contributions you are most proud of and how you leveraged the APS Principles to achieve them.	
Delivering Consistent, High-Quality Member Support
I maintained strong performance in handling member inquiries with accuracy, empathy, and professionalism. By applying APS principles such as member first and operating with integrity, I ensured every interaction was clear, timely, and solution focused. This helped build trust and reduce repeat contact.

Enhancing Team Communication and Reducing Member Miscommunication
I took initiative to communicate clearly with team leads, managers, and peers especially when members were waiting for escalations or guidance. Using the one APS principle, I made sure members never felt ignored by keeping them informed, setting expectations, and collaborating with teammates to deliver consistent and professional service. This helped prevent misunderstandings and supported smoother workflow across the team.

2. Think back to your development in 2024, share 1-2 examples of areas that you grew based on feedback from your leader and/or others.	
Improving Adherence and Schedule Alignment
One of the main areas of feedback I received was strengthening my adherence to scheduled breaks, lunches, and log-in times. Throughout 2024, I made a conscious effort to better manage my time and plans to stay aligned with schedule expectations. This helped support team coverage, reduce gaps in service, and ensured members experienced fewer delays during high-volume periods.

Reducing Call Handling Time While Maintaining Quality
Another focus area was improving my average handling time. With guidance from my leader and team leader, I worked on simplifying my call flow, staying more focused during interactions, and using resources more efficiently. I learned how to resolve issues faster without sacrificing accuracy or empathy, which improved both my productivity and the member experience.

 
Year End Leader Discussion Topics and Feedback
2025::Scoticia Osborne.copilotResponse:

Scoticia’s performance was solidly on track for the year, with several areas where she exceeded expectations and delivered meaningful impact. Member experience metrics were another standout, including Rep Satisfaction at 87.5% against a goal of 80.0%, Overall Sentiment at 91.2% against a goal of 88.0%, and Positive Word at 88.3% against a goal of 86.0%. She also met all major APS annual goals, including safety accuracy, emergency hazard handling, phishing avoidance, and maintaining zero substantiated complaints. Alyssa's notes. -

1. Think back to your business goals for 2024. Highlight the 2-3 business contributions you are most proud of and how you leveraged the APS Principles to achieve them.	
I am most proud of meeting the goals of AHT, Work Ready in Seconds, Adherence and Manages Emotion. I am new to the Company and have worked hard to meet these goal. Through Phase 3 and Phase 4 training and transition, I have leveraged the APS Principle of Empower Each Other as our team continues to encourage one another to learn to tasks important to the function of APS and customer satisfaction. 

In my time here I've handled 6,523 calls and as of 10/28 my AHT is 27 and maintained an adherence of 91.71%. I make an effort to manage my emotions while on calls with an updated score of 95.80% while avoiding negative word choice 86.80% of the time.


2. Think back to your development in 2024, share 1-2 examples of areas that you grew based on feedback from your leader and/or others.	
One area they wanted me to make improvements in is my AHT time and my  ACW and Avoiding negative word choice. I have also tried to take more initiative in my calls by using empowerment where allowed.

2025::Caylie Chirumbolo.managerContext:
Caylie's words. Incorporate some of this into the review.

1. Think back to your business goals for 2024. Highlight the 2-3 business contributions you are most proud of and how you leveraged the APS Principles to achieve them.	
This year I caught a customer account that had not been set up correctly for service. The customer was wanting to start service at an apartment unit but instead it had been set up as an apartment building. The customer had been trying for awhile to get the issue resolved & nobody before me was able to get this fixed. By noticing the discrepancy & taking quick action, I was able to prevent a service interruption & ensure the customers service was completed accurately. I leveraged our customer focus & integrity principles by double-checking details, communicating clearly, & taking ownership until the issue was fully resolved. This experience reminded me of the impact careful attention can have on a customers experience.

During the government shutdown, many customers have been anxious about bills & looking for clarity on their options. I worked to provide calm, patient, & consistent support by listening to their concerns, offering solutions, & helping them navigate available resources. This demonstrated adaptability, as every call was different and required flexibility as well as collaboration, as I worked with my team to to stay aligned on guidance while keeping customers informed. I'm proud of how I was able to maintain empathy & professionalism during a very stressful time for our community.

Overall, I feel I have leveraged the APS principles throughout the year by staying committed to accuracy, compassion, & service. I'm looking forward to continuing to grow in 2026 & expanding my contributions to the team. 
2. Think back to your development in 2024, share 1-2 examples of areas that you grew based on feedback from your leader and/or others.	
In 2024, I experienced meaningful growth in tow key areas thanks to feedback from my leaders. Earlier in the year, I received feedback encouraging me to trust my judgement more when navigating complex customer issues. Since then, I 've grown in my ability to assess situations, use the tools available, & make confident decisions without second-guessing myself. This has helped me resolve calls more efficiently & feel more comfortable taking ownership of unique situations.

I was also encouraged to slow down & focus on delivering information clearly, especially when customers were stressed or overwhelmed. I took this to heart & have been working on staying calm, pacing my explanations, & ensuring customers fully understand their options. This improvement has strengthened my interactions & has been reflected in positive customer interactions/feedback.
2025::Caylie Chirumbolo.copilotResponse:
Box 1 - Significant Accomplishments:
Caylie’s overall performance was on track for 2025, and she delivered strong results across several key business and APS principle areas. She exceeded multiple performance targets, including Schedule Adherence at 93.7% vs the 93.0% goal, Average Handle Time at 399 seconds vs the 440 second goal, Hold Time at 14 seconds vs the 30 second goal, Rep Satisfaction at 82.1% vs the 80.0% goal, First Call Resolution at 82.3% vs the 70.0% goal, and Overall Sentiment at 91.5% vs the 88.0% goal. She also met all annual APS goals, including 100% accuracy on Emergency Safety Hazard calls, 0 ACC substantiated complaints, and 0 phishing email clicks. Beyond the metrics, she demonstrated strong customer focus and integrity by catching and correcting a complex service setup issue that prevented a customer interruption, and she showed adaptability and compassion while supporting customers during the government shutdown. Her growth in trusting her judgment and communicating with clarity strengthened her interactions and contributed to consistently positive customer experiences.

Box 2 - Future Improvement Areas:
Two areas would benefit from continued focus in the coming year. Transfer rate was 23.9% vs the 6.0% goal, and reducing unnecessary transfers will help streamline the customer experience and build confidence in resolving more situations independently. After Call Work averaged 119 seconds vs the 60 second goal, and continued refinement of workflow organization and wrap-up efficiency will support stronger overall productivity.

Mid-Year Check-In
Every employee should expect a mid-year conversation with their leader.

Comments are required for an off-track status rating and optional for On-track status to highlight focus areas for the remainder of the year.

Mid Year Status	
On-Track
Comments:	
Taylor is currently On track for this review period, successfully meeting four out of five primary driver performance metrics. The only area slightly off target is her CX Overall Rep Score, which is trailing by 8%. Despite this, Taylor has demonstrated strong overall performance and continues to be a valuable asset to the team.

Key Strengths and Contributions:

Communication Excellence: Taylor’s communication skills have been a standout strength this year. Her ability to convey complex or sensitive information to customers with clarity and empathy is reflected in her high Sentiment score of 94.40%, which is well above target.
Leadership and Initiative: Taylor has taken on additional responsibilities, including serving as a backup Team Lead (TL). Her deep understanding of the CXC process and consistent performance made her a reliable choice for this role. She also began handling call escalations and secured additional access to deregister accounts, pull Res Docs, and become ECE trained.
Professional Development: Taylor proactively joined a networking group to increase her visibility and build community within the organization. She also secured a six-month rotation as a backup TL supporting the TTS team and is currently piloting the new escalation line, providing her with valuable exposure and growth opportunities.
Primary Metrics:

Adherence: 93.17% – On Target

Reliability: 1.29 – On Target
